title: Chapter 5 Quiz: Network Devices & Topologies
description: Test your knowledge of hubs, switches, routers, firewalls, and Wi-Fi fundamentals!

questions.question: What is the primary disadvantage of a Bus topology?
options: Single point of failure (break in cable takes down network)
questions.explanation: In a Bus topology, a break anywhere in the backbone cable disrupts the entire segment.

questions.question: Which topology provides the highest level of redundancy and fault tolerance?
questions.explanation: Mesh topology provides multiple redundant paths, ensuring network availability even if several links fail.

questions.question: At which OSI layer does a standard Ethernet Switch operate?
questions.explanation: Switches make forwarding decisions based on MAC addresses, which are Layer 2 addresses.

questions.question: What does a Hub do when it receives a signal on one port?
options: Forwards it only to the destination port
Floods it out all other ports
Drops it if the MAC address is unknown
Routes it to the default gateway
questions.explanation: Hubs are repeaters; they regenerate and forward the signal to all ports except the incoming one.

questions.question: A switch breaks up a Collision Domain but does NOT break up a Broadcast Domain by default.
questions.explanation: True. Each switch port is its own collision domain, but all ports are in the same broadcast domain (VLAN 1 by default).

questions.question: What is the name of the table a switch uses to map MAC addresses to ports?
questions.explanation: The Content Addressable Memory (CAM) table stores MAC-to-port mappings.

questions.question: A switch receives a frame destined for a MAC address not in its CAM table. What action does it take?
options: Flood the frame out all ports except the receiving port
Send an ARP request to find the MAC
questions.explanation: This is 'Unknown Unicast Flooding'. The switch hopes the destination will respond.

questions.question: The process of a switch learning MAC addresses by examining the source MAC of incoming frames is called ______.
questions.explanation: Switches dynamically build their CAM table by inspecting source MAC addresses.

questions.question: At which OSI layer does a Router operate?
questions.explanation: Routers operate at Layer 3 (Network Layer) and forward packets based on IP addresses.

questions.question: What is the function of the 'Default Gateway' on a host?
options: The MAC address of the local switch
The IP address of the router used to reach other networks
The IP address of the DHCP server
questions.explanation: The Default Gateway is the exit point for traffic destined for a different IP subnet.

questions.question: Which routing table entry will a router choose if multiple entries match a destination IP?
options: The one with the shortest prefix length
The one with the longest prefix length (most specific)
The one with the lowest metric
The first one in the list
questions.explanation: Routers use Longest Prefix Match to select the most specific route.

questions.question: What is a Layer 3 Switch?
options: A switch that only handles VLANs
A switch capable of routing IP packets in hardware
questions.explanation: Layer 3 switches can perform IP routing at wire speed using ASICs, ideal for inter-VLAN routing.

questions.question: Which type of firewall tracks the state of TCP connections (e.g., SYN, SYN-ACK, ESTABLISHED)?
questions.explanation: Stateful firewalls maintain a state table and allow return traffic only for established connections.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between an IDS and an IPS?
options: IDS is hardware, IPS is software
IDS detects and alerts; IPS detects and blocks
IDS works at Layer 3, IPS works at Layer 7
questions.explanation: IDS is passive (alarm). IPS is inline (prevention).

questions.question: What does 'DMZ' stand for in network security?
questions.explanation: DMZ is a neutral network segment where public-facing servers are placed, isolated from the internal LAN.

questions.question: A Web Application Firewall (WAF) is designed to protect against attacks like SQL Injection and Cross-Site Scripting.
questions.explanation: True. WAFs operate at Layer 7 and understand HTTP/HTTPS to block application-layer attacks.

questions.question: Which Wi-Fi frequency band offers better penetration through walls but suffers from more interference?
questions.explanation: 2.4 GHz has longer wavelengths that penetrate obstacles better, but it's crowded with microwaves, Bluetooth, etc.

questions.question: The name of a wireless network as seen by clients is called the ______.
questions.explanation: SSID (Service Set Identifier) is the human-readable network name.

questions.question: Which wireless security standard is considered broken and should NEVER be used?
questions.explanation: WEP is cryptographically broken and can be cracked in minutes.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of WPA2-Enterprise over WPA2-Personal?
options: It provides unique credentials per user (via RADIUS)
questions.explanation: Enterprise mode uses 802.1X authentication, eliminating the shared password vulnerability of PSK.

questions.question: What is a Deauthentication Attack?
options: Spoofing management frames to disconnect a client
questions.explanation: Deauth attacks exploit unencrypted management frames to force clients to disconnect.

questions.question: What protocol is used between a Wireless LAN Controller (WLC) and Lightweight Access Points (LWAPs)?
questions.explanation: CAPWAP (Control and Provisioning of Wireless Access Points) manages and configures APs.

questions.question: What is the function of Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on a switch?
questions.explanation: STP detects and disables redundant paths to prevent broadcast storms and MAC table instability.

questions.question: An unmanaged switch is suitable for enterprise network core deployments.
questions.explanation: False. Unmanaged switches lack security features, VLAN support, and monitoring capabilities.

questions.question: The route `0.0.0.0/0` in a routing table is known as the ______ route.
questions.explanation: The default route matches all destinations and is used when no more specific route exists.
